summary:
The bug was that the API accepted an adjustment that would drive a product’s inventory quantity below 0.  
Fixed by adding validation in two places:

1. routes.ts  
   • After reading the body, but before calling `adjustQuantity`, reject any adjustment that would leave the quantity negative and return HTTP 400.

2. inventory.ts  
   • Inside `adjustQuantity`, throw an error if the new quantity would become negative, safeguarding any direct use of the class.

Both changes ensure the server never allows impossible (negative) inventory states.  
Running `./test.sh` now prints

PASS: Input validation correctly prevents impossible inventory states
